data.title: As a practitioner, /dso:brainstorm blocks the Phase 2 approval gate on unresolved access contradictions and propagates confirmation-token requirements
data.description:
## Description

**What**: At the brainstorm Phase 2 approval gate, inspect the rendered External Dependencies block for contradictions between handling and claude_has_access fields, and refuse to present the gate until the practitioner resolves them. When a dependency omits verification_command, propagate its justification and mark the dependency as requiring a confirmation-token exchange at sprint pause time.

**Why**: Advances SC2. Prevents epics from being approved with dependencies whose automation-vs-access state is inherently contradictory, which would otherwise produce stories that fail silently at sprint time.

**Scope**:
- IN: Approval-gate inspection of External Dependencies block for contradictions; refusal with diagnostic when handling=claude_auto AND claude_has_access IN {no, unknown}; propagation of justification + confirmation-token marker into the block's downstream-consumed shape when verification_command is omitted. Flag-gated by planning.external_dependency_block_enabled.
- OUT: Sprint-side token collection or audit comment writing (Story F); preplanning-side interpretation of tokens (Story D).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the Phase 2 approval gate refuses to present whenever a block entry has handling=claude_auto AND claude_has_access IN {no, unknown}; the diagnostic names the entry and the required resolution (flip handling to user_manual or confirm claude_has_access=yes)
  Satisfies: "/dso:brainstorm blocks the Phase 2 approval gate on unresolved contradictions. When a dependency is declared handling=claude_auto + claude_has_access=no, the gate refuses to present until the practitioner resolves it"
- When this story is complete, a block entry with an omitted verification_command carries a non-empty justification field and a confirmation_token_required marker readable by downstream skills
  Satisfies: "When verification_command is omitted, sprint's pause handshake requires the user to type a confirmation token and logs it to the ticket as an audit trail."
- When this story is complete, with the flag off neither the contradiction refusal nor the token-marker propagation activates (gate renders as before)
  Satisfies: "The entire feature sits behind a config flag ... When off, all four skills behave identically to current baseline."
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for the contradiction detector (covers both claude_has_access=no and =unknown) and for the justification/token-marker propagation

## Considerations

- [Testing] Gate refusal should be testable via a brainstorm skill harness that injects a contradiction-containing block and asserts the refusal diagnostic without reaching user prompt
- [Maintainability] Contradiction rule table lives alongside the schema contract from Story A — keep rule additions centralized
- [Security] Track which user-provided fields are allowed to be omitted with justification vs. always required — design so new required fields default to present-with-justification, not silently optional

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
